content: If $$0< a<5,0< b<5$$ and $$\frac{x^2+5}{2}=x-2[\cos (a+b x)]$$ is satisfied for atleast one real $$x$$, then the least value of $$\frac{a+b}{\pi}$$ is equal to
explanation:
Given equation is

$$\begin{array}{ll} & \frac{x^2+5}{2}=x-2 \cos (a+b x) \\ \Rightarrow & x^2+5=2 x-4 \cos (a+b x) \\ \Rightarrow & x^2-2 x+5=-4 \cos (a+b x) \\ \Rightarrow & (x-1)^2+4=-4 \cos (a+b x) \end{array}$$

Equality holds only when $$x=1$$

$$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad & -4 \cos (a+b)=4 \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \cos (a+b) =-1 \end{aligned}$$

So, least value of $$a+b=\pi$$

$$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{a+b}{\pi}=1$$

content: The roots of the equation $$\cos x+\sqrt{3} \sin x=2 \cos 2 x$$, are
explanation:
As $$|\cos x+\sqrt{3} \sin x| \leq \sqrt{1^2+(\sqrt{3})^2} \leq 2$$

For the solution to exist $$|2 \cos 2 x| \leq 2$$

$$\Rightarrow|\cos 2 x| \leq 1$$, which is true.

Now, $$\cos x+\sqrt{3} \sin x=2 \cos 2 x$$

$$\begin{array}{ll} \Rightarrow & \frac{1}{2} \cos x+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \sin x=\cos 2 x \\ \Rightarrow & \cos \frac{\pi}{3} \cos x+\sin \frac{\pi}{3} \sin x=\cos 2 x \\ \Rightarrow & \cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)=\cos 2 x \\ \Rightarrow & x-\frac{\pi}{3}=2 n \pi \pm 2 x \end{array}$$

On taking positive sign, we get

$$\begin{aligned} x & =\frac{\pi}{3}+2 n \pi+2 x \\ \Rightarrow \quad x & =-\left(\frac{\pi}{3}+2 n \pi\right), n \in Z \end{aligned}$$

On taking negative sign, we get

$$\begin{aligned} & x=\frac{\pi}{3}+2 n \pi-2 x \Rightarrow 3 x=2 n \pi+\frac{\pi}{3} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & x=\frac{2 n \pi}{3}+\frac{\pi}{9}, n \in Z \end{aligned}$$

Hence, the roots of the given equation are $$-\left(\frac{\pi}{3}+2 n \pi\right)$$ and $$\left(\frac{2 n \pi}{3}+\frac{\pi}{9}\right) \forall n \in Z$$
